articles.title: Israel and Iran ramp up attacks as Trump insists Tehran wants a deal
articles.snippet: The war in the Middle East ramped up on Thursday as Israel launched a wave of strikes targeting Iranian infrastructure, and Iran fired rounds of missiles at central Israel.

articles.title: 'The era of impunity is over': What next for big tech after landmark social media verdict?
articles.snippet: The ruling could be the beginning of the end of social media as we know it, writes the BBC Technology Editor Zoe Kleinman.

articles.title: Trump's Threats to Europe Put Its Leaders in a Double Bind Over Iran
articles.snippet: European politicians risk angering their voters if they join America's war. Yet they could also face domestic upheaval if they take no action to reopen shipping routes that Iran has blocked and ease an energy crisis.

articles.title: Some Judges See Risks in Fiery Opinions Warning of Threats to Democracy
articles.snippet: Federal judges are weighing strategies for how to respond to the high stakes, anonymous threats and politicized atmosphere of the Trump era.

articles.title: British forces will be able to board Russian shadow fleet ships in UK waters
articles.snippet: The move will "starve Putin's war machine", Starmer says as he arrives in Finland for a military summit.

articles.title: War with Iran disrupts fertilizer exports as U.S. farmers prepare for planting season
articles.snippet: Gulf states are major fertilizer producers, and the war with Iran has triggered a 25% price hike just as struggling U.S. farmers are planting corn.

articles.title: After a decade as a Yale hospital janitor, she is now a doctor there
articles.snippet: "I still can't believe it," said Shay Taylor-Allen, who landed a residency at Yale New Haven Hospital, where she was born and spent most of her adult life on the cleaning staff.

articles.title: It's Equal Pay Day. Women have lost ground for the second year in a row
articles.snippet: The annual observance marks how far into the new year women must work to make what men earned in the previous year. This year, it's March 26, a day later than it was in 2025.

articles.title: Trump's Iran strategy is to pursue two off-ramps at once
articles.snippet: The White House insists the president is dictating events, but even some Republicans are frustrated by mixed messaging about his war aims.

articles.title: A $50 billion fund to help rural hospitals could actually lead to closures and cuts
articles.snippet: States are rolling out plans for their share of a $50 billion fund established by Congress to improve rural health care. In some states, the money may provoke rural hospitals to cut services.
